codeReview.summary: The feature largely delivers the required public APIs, layout shell, and homepage sections, but it contains a blocking navigation regression: PublicLayout user links target non-existent /users/* routes instead of the app's actual auth/account routes.

issues.description: User nav links are wired to /users/login, /users/register, /users/account, and /users/profile/:username, but router definitions in app/apps/client/src/App.tsx only define /login, /register, /forgot-password, and /admin/account (no /users/* routes). This causes Login/Register/My Account links in the new public layout to lead to 404 routes.

Sidebar category links use `/categories/${cat.catUri}` while this commit's router only defines `/categories/:slug` (single segment). If catUri contains nested paths (e.g. `php/basics`, as seeded in app/apps/server/src/__tests__/public.test.ts), child category links become unmatched routes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared docs do not define canonical frontend public auth/account route paths, which likely contributed to route mismatch in the new layout (/users/* links vs existing /login and /register routes).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md describe API route conventions but not frontend auth URL conventions; mismatch visible between app/apps/client/src/components/PublicLayout.tsx:212-218 and app/apps/client/src/App.tsx:45-54.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker procedure's manual verification guidance is broad and did not force click-through verification of newly added navigation links, allowing broken href targets to pass despite extensive tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md step 8 requires manual verification but does not explicitly require validating each new link target; handoff interactiveChecks mention nav state checks but not clicking Login/Register/My Account destinations.

summary: Fail: found a blocking user-navigation routing mismatch in PublicLayout (/users/* links do not match app routes), plus a non-blocking nested-category link routing risk.
